task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: 11956 Incorrect job detected

section.title: Symptom
section.p: A job is submitted with the RIP. The data is transferred correctly, but the job does not appear on the operator panel of the printer.
The same occurs if you submit a job with the PrintJobSubmitter tool.
A reboot does not solve the problem.

section.title: Possible cause
section.p: The job submitted to the printer was not within specifications or has been corrupted during transport.
The job was therefore discarded during processing by the controller software. No document was printed.
If such a job is submitted, it might block any next job: if you submit a correct job it also might not appear on the operator panel.
The printer will not display any message. Printing is not possible anymore.
Aborting a job in the RIP, also results in an incomplete job in the controller.
It is strongly advised to not abort the job in the RIP, but to delete the job on the operator panel of the printer.

note.p: The current software releases provides no information to the user when such an error occurs.
The job does not appear on the Operator panel and no information is given to the user about the reason why the job was rejected.

section.title: Tests/Checks
section.p: In PRISMAservice in the product app; under the section Analyze, technical views standard the view "60 RIP input warnings" is present.
In this view the reported 11956 warnings are listed with the specific details.
Alternatively this information can also be found in the datalog.xml. Open the Datalog.xml file and search for error code 11956 occurrences. See : How to get the datalog file
The ErrorDetails parameter value provides additional information about the error cause.
If such an error is present, then the controller has detected an error in the job which can be corrected with the RIP; see the examples below.
If such an error is not present, execute the following checks:

li.p: submit a correct job (a job you printed before).
reboot the printer and submit a correct job.

section.p: If in both situations the RIP successfully transfers the data to the printer, but the printer does nothing (no info on the operator panel, no printing), then you need to reinstall the printer software (with keep settings). See : 11 How to do a system software installation on a non-empty disk

section.title: Some example ErrorDetails and their solution

note.p: An option is now available in the Settings Editor to remove all print jobs. This option can be used in the exceptional cases in which a job is blocking the system.
The option can be found here : Support > Troubleshooting > Printer restart and job removal > Remove all jobs when the system restarts

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: File is corrupted (received file size: (file size) bytes)

entry.p: The file received by the printer does not have the expected size (indicated in the job footer section of the data).
The job transmission between RIP station and printer was probably interrupted before completion.
Check if the job transmission was manually cancelled by operator
If the issue remains, test the connection with the printer using the DotPrint Job Submitter tool.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: Preview check failed

entry.p: The preview thumbnail data embedded in the job are not present or corrupted.
Check if the job transmission was manually cancelled. by the operator (see note).
Save the job and check with DotPrint Job Viewer tool if the thumbnail can be previewed.
If the issue remains, test the connection with the printer using the DotPrint Job Submitter tool.

note.p: For Onyx jobs, preview thumbnail data are located just after the file header (before the image data).
For Caldera jobs, they are located at the end of the file (after the image data).

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: The job footer command is not valid

entry.p: Jobs created with RIPs drivers include a job footer section detected by the printer software to check all job data were received.
Check if the job transmission was manually cancelled by the operator .
Save the job and check with DotPrint Job Viewer tool if the job footer command is present.
If the issue remains, test the connection with the printer using the DotPrint Job Submitter tool.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: Roll width is not within range 279400 <= (Roll width) <= 1625600

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an incorrect Roll width value in the header.
Check if the RIP driver version matches with the printers software version.
Upgrade the RIP driver or the printer software so they match.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: MediaType [(mediatype ID)] is not a valid GUID

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an incorrect Media Type ID in the header.

li.p: Click the new button added in the RIP UI to force media synchronization between the RIP and the printer.
Click the button in the RIP UI to force media synchronization between the RIP and the printer.
Change media type properties to the required media.
Remove the media type with the indicated mediatype ID before syncing the media from onyx.
3) Change media type properties to the required media

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: The leading edge (2000000 micrometers) is bigger than 1 meter

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
Edit the submitted job in the RIP and use a correct setting.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: .BATCH file with batch-ID=... job=... replaced the file with the same batch-ID and seq-number of job

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
Check/adapt the workflow configuration in the RIP together with PRISMAGuide

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: .PRINT file with batch-ID=... job=... replaced the file with the same batch-ID and seq-number of job

entry.p: This is caused by a multilayer configuration error in the RIP.
Check/adapt the workflow configuration in the RIP together with PRISMAGuide

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: BitsPerChannel 0 not supported.

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
Edit the submitted job in the RIP and use the correct setting. Resubmit the modified job. If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: File is corrupted (received file size …)

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
Edit the submitted job in the RIP and use a correct setting.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: Invalid file: .PRINT file without layers

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with incorrect multilayer settings in the header.
Edit the submitted job in the RIP and use a correct setting.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: Invalid file: Actual file size [..] not equal to expected job file size [..]

entry.p: The Controller received a job that does not have the correct size.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: Invalid file: Data at position ... in the stream is not a TileHeader

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with incorrect parameter value in the header.
Edit the submitted job in the RIP and use the correct setting.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: Invalid file: Error at position 0: only … bytes read, whereas the tile header indicates ….

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
Edit the submitted job in the RIP and use the correct setting.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: Invalid file: No job header and no batch header

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
Edit the submitted job in the RIP and use a correct setting.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: Invalid file: No TileHeader can be excerpted from the dotprint file at line 0

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
Edit the submitted job in the RIP and use a correct setting.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: Invalid file: Severe error

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
Edit the submitted job in the RIP and use a correct setting.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: Invalid file: This dotprint is not encoded with tiles.

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with incorrect data.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: Invalid file: Unable to decompress tile at [X;Y]=[0;0].

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
Edit the submitted job in the RIP and use a correct setting.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: MediaTypeId [...] is not defined

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
Edit the submitted job in the RIP and use a correct setting.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: Not all channelMappings are present in the data for layer 1: 5

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: NumberOfCopies 0 out of range. Must be between 1 and 10000

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: xxx is NOT supported

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: PrintModeId Found unexpected channel(s): W

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
The indicated printmode does not support white.
Check if the used media profile and printmode match with the machine capabilities.
Edit the submitted job in the RIP and use a correct setting.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: ErrorDetails = Incorrect job detected: PrintModeId too many layers

entry.p: The RIP sent a job with an out-of-range parameter value in the header.
The selected printmode does not support the indicated layers.
Check if the used media profile and printmode match with the machine capabilities.
Edit the submitted job in the RIP and use a correct setting.
If the issue remains, report it to the RIP provider.

section.title: What the software checks
section.p: The software checks if the following job header parameters values are within the allowed range:
